data.label: Renderer UX
data.description: Owns the existing React/Electron renderer surface: canvas authoring, dispatch controls, assistant panel, files, preview, and terminal layout.
data.systemPrompt: You own the existing renderer implementation in src/renderer/src/**. Extend and refine the ReactFlow canvas, layout panels, dispatch UX, and assistant integration without breaking the current workflow. Treat window.electron as the stable boundary and keep Electron or filesystem concerns out of renderer code.

data.label: Desktop App
data.description: Existing React renderer that operators use to design canvases and manage live agent sessions.
data.specs: This is the current UI layer in src/renderer/src/, not a greenfield app. It already contains DirectoryGate, the ReactFlow canvas, TopNav, Sidebar, AssistantPanel, DispatchModal, FilesPanel, PreviewPanel, and TerminalPanel. Changes here should preserve the existing operator workflow while improving canvas editing, dispatch ergonomics, terminal management, and assistant-driven updates through the window.electron API.

data.label: Electron Platform
data.description: Owns the existing preload bridge, BrowserWindow lifecycle, filesystem IPC, canvas persistence, and local watcher plumbing.
data.systemPrompt: You own the current Electron platform layer in src/preload/index.ts and src/main/index.ts. Evolve the contextBridge API carefully, preserve contextIsolation semantics, and keep canvas or filesystem concerns centralized in the main process instead of leaking them into the renderer.

data.label: IPC Bridge
data.description: Established preload contract that exposes the allowed Electron surface as window.electron.
data.specs: This bridge already exists in src/preload/index.ts and is the renderer's stable boundary into Electron. It currently exposes file access, canvas load/save/watch, component scanning, graph execution, assistant controls, terminal streaming, layout persistence, and per-zone session APIs. Extend it carefully, keep the contract typed, and avoid leaking raw Node or Electron primitives into renderer code.

data.label: Main Process
data.description: Current Electron shell that owns window lifecycle, IPC registration, and native integration points.
data.specs: This is the existing src/main/index.ts entrypoint. It already creates the BrowserWindow, registers filesystem and canvas IPC handlers, manages popout windows, starts and stops canvas watchers, and routes dispatch and zone operations into src/main/terminals.ts. Work here should preserve app boot, context isolation, and the current IPC contract while tightening responsibilities or adding new native capabilities.

data.label: Canvas Store
data.description: Persistent architect-canvas.json file plus the file-watch path that keeps the renderer in sync.
data.specs: The canvas persistence path already exists. architect-canvas.json in the project root is the source of truth, and the main process watches it to emit canvas:changed events back to the renderer. Changes should preserve live reload semantics, migration compatibility, and the current save/load flow rather than replacing the storage model.

data.label: Dispatch Coordination
data.description: Owns the current dispatch pipeline: graph indexing, prompt synthesis, coordinator spawning, and the file-backed task handoff between zones.
data.systemPrompt: You own the existing dispatch pipeline in src/main/terminals.ts. Maintain and extend runGraph and runZone around geometry-based zone membership, prompt generation, ARCHITECT workspace setup, task file watching, and the multi-zone coordinator flow. Improve the current orchestration model rather than replacing it wholesale.

data.label: PTY Orchestrator
data.description: Existing runtime manager that owns live CLI processes and terminal streams.
data.specs: This logic already lives in src/main/terminals.ts. It resolves installed agent binaries, builds runtime-specific CLI arguments, spawns node-pty sessions, broadcasts terminal output to renderer and popout windows, handles input and resize events, and tracks completion markers. Work here should extend or harden the current orchestration behavior instead of replacing the session model.

data.label: Overseer
data.description: Current architect-agent coordinator used during multi-zone dispatches.
data.specs: In multi-zone runs the app already spawns an architect-agent session that reads ARCHITECT/manifest.json and the generated prompt files, writes one task file per zone, and coordinates downstream handoffs from zone status logs. Treat this as an existing coordination mechanism that can be refined, not a new agent pattern that needs to be invented.

data.label: Task Queue
data.description: Current file-backed coordination channel under ARCHITECT/tasks and ARCHITECT/outputs.
data.specs: The queue is already implemented as filesystem handoff, not a separate broker. The Architect coordinator writes task markdown files, the watcher in src/main/terminals.ts releases zones when those files appear, and zone agents write progress back into ARCHITECT/outputs. Changes here should respect the existing file-based protocol and improve its reliability or clarity.

data.label: Zone Runtime Fleet
data.description: Owns the existing reusable zone sessions, worker runtimes, skill ingestion, and ARCHITECT workspace artifacts consumed by live agents.
data.systemPrompt: You own the current per-zone execution surface: session capture and resume, worker session identity, skill file ingestion, and the ARCHITECT workspace directories that agents read and write while working. Preserve existing resume and coordination behavior while improving reliability, clarity, and runtime support.

data.label: Agent Pool
data.description: Existing pool of per-zone CLI sessions across the supported runtimes.
data.specs: The app already knows how to spawn or resume Claude, Codex, Gemini, and OpenCode sessions for each zone. It persists captured session ids, keeps stable zone identities, and lets the UI resume long-lived agents. Changes should preserve resume semantics and runtime-specific behavior while improving durability, observability, or runtime coverage.

data.label: Skills Library
data.description: Current skill source made up of builtin and custom SKILL.md files.
data.specs: Zone prompts already inline skill content from repo-root builtin skill folders and from custom file references. This component represents that existing prompt-enrichment path. Extend it by improving discovery, resolution, or guardrails, but keep compatibility with the current builtin: and custom: reference scheme.

data.label: Agent Workspace
data.description: Existing ARCHITECT/ coordination directory created inside each opened project.
data.specs: This workspace already contains manifest.json, prompt files, tasks, outputs, saved sessions, and dispatch history for the current project. It is coordination-only and intentionally separate from real project code. Changes should preserve that contract while improving how dispatch artifacts are organized, inspected, or resumed.
